context: A lean 8-developer startup needs to onboard two new full-stack engineers context switching quickly.
approach: Golden Rule Setup: Local environment spins up in Docker in 30 minutes. New hires pair with the CTO on day 2 to deploy a tiny CSS fix to production (TTFC < 2 days).
outcome: Immediate psychological safety and velocity. No complex 30-day plan needed due to extreme proximity.

context: A 25-developer team grew fast. New hires are taking 45 days to become autonomous because documentation is outdated and seniors are too busy.
approach: Implement the 'Structured 30-Day Mentorship'. Mandate 2 buddy sessions per week, assign a controlled production bug in Week 1, and a medium feature requiring architectural design in Week 4.
outcome: Simulated Seniority in week 4 proves independence. TTFP drops to 8 days. True autonomy reached by day 25.

context: A 100-developer traditional enterprise has massive 'tribal knowledge'. Onboarding consists of 'read this wiki' which is 2 years out of date.
approach: Standardized Enterprise Onboarding. Force strict Quality Gates to prevent overwhelmed juniors from breaking builds. Mandate centralized logging architecture overviews within the first 3 days.
outcome: Protects the codebase via CI/CD gates while forcing the organization to update 100% of ADRs. Onboarding becomes safer.

context: A 60-developer modern SaaS with a dedicated platform team wants to optimize the developer experience.
approach: Data-Driven Onboarding. Instrument GitHub and JIRA to automatically track TTFC and TTFP. Deploy Friction Surveys in week 2. Analyze PR friction heatmaps to identify confusing modules.
outcome: Treats onboarding as a distinct Product. Continuous IDP refinement reduces onboarding time by 40% year-over-year.
